etymology.description: 記(記) (jì, "record; remember") and 得(得) (dé, "obtain; get") together convey the idea of "successfully recording in one's mind," thus meaning "to remember."
etymology.interpretation: This combination makes sense because 記(記) (jì, "record; remember") implies the act of noting information mentally, while 得(得) (dé, "obtain; get") indicates the successful acquisition of this memory.

examples.chinese: 请记得锁门(請記得鎖門)。
examples.english: Please remember to lock the door.

examples.chinese: 你还记得那次旅行吗(你還記得那次旅行嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ hái jìde nà cì lǚxíng ma?
examples.english: Do you still remember that trip?

examples.chinese: 他让我记得带伞(他讓我記得帶傘)。
examples.pinyin: Tā ràng wǒ jìde dài sǎn.
examples.english: He asked me to remember to bring an umbrella.
